Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Criminal Code (criminal interest rate)
Identification.RunningHead: An Act to amend the Criminal Code (criminal interest rate)

Provision.Text: This enactment amends the Criminal Code to reduce the criminal rate of interest from sixty per cent to the Bank of Canadaâs overnight rate plus twenty per cent.
Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Subsection.Text: The definition criminal rate in subsection 347(2) of the Criminal Code is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: criminal rate means an effective annual rate of interest calculated in accordance with generally accepted actuarial practices and principles that exceeds the Bank of Canadaâs overnight rate plus twenty per cent on the credit advanced under an agreement or arrangement; (taux criminel)

criminal rate means an effective annual rate of interest calculated in accordance with generally accepted actuarial practices and principles that exceeds sixty per cent on the credit advanced under an agreement or arrangement; (taux criminel)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 347(2) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: Bank of Canadaâs overnight rate, in relation to credit advanced, means the Bank of Canadaâs overnight rate on the day on which the agreement or arrangement under which the credit is or is to be advanced is entered into or renewed; (taux de financement Ã un jour de la Banque du Canada)

Section.Text: This Act comes into force 60 days after the day on which it receives royal assent.
